observed_failure.surface: SkillGuard v0.3.1 GitHub Actions full Windows suite
observed_failure.prior_local_claim: the complete local regression and release validation were green
observed_failure.observed_result: Windows CI reported seven failures while both Ubuntu jobs passed: one physical temporary directory appeared under both 8.3 short and long spellings, a stage-root link lost its reparse identity after resolution, and isolated replay tests consulted the runner's undeclared user installation root

classification.owner: portable content boundary, target installation stage verification, and release TestMesh
classification.error_signature: RUNNER~1 and runneradmin spellings compare unequal, reparse stage resolves before rejection, or replay reaches an undeclared real CODEX_HOME

root_cause_backpropagation.supported_cause: the model covered portable relative paths, long-path I/O, and installation projection bytes, but did not distinguish physical path identity from lexical spelling at all Windows boundaries or require every external-binding replay fixture to own its CODEX_HOME lookup
root_cause_backpropagation.would_have_failed_if: the release model had included a Windows runner whose temporary root is returned once as an 8.3 alias and once as a long path, a valid directory symlink presented as a stage root, and a clean runner with no user-level SkillGuard installation
root_cause_backpropagation.new_model_item: same physical root under supported Windows aliases must preserve containment and role classification; final-component reparse identity must be checked before resolution; isolated replay must bind every external root lookup to fixture-owned evidence
root_cause_backpropagation.owner_code_contract: portable_content.relative_member_path + target_installation.verify_target_stage + test_mesh replay fixture bindings

generalized_repair.rule: Canonicalize existing Windows ancestors to their long physical spelling while preserving the final lexical component, reject reparse stage roots before following them, compare physical test paths by same-file identity, and inject the fixture CODEX_HOME before replay currentness lookup.
generalized_repair.observed_regression: the original seven failing tests are the first affected regression shard
generalized_repair.same_class_regression: a dedicated Windows test presents the same member under short-root and long-candidate spellings and still classifies .pytest_cache as generic transient runtime content
generalized_repair.negative_boundary: an actual escape or final-component link remains unsafe; no compatibility reader, alternate install root, skipped check, or weakened invariant is introduced

required_revalidation: current FlowGuard model checks and SkillGuard contract compilation
Windows and Ubuntu GitHub Actions before the corrective release

revalidation_evidence.original_failure_shard: 17 passed, 2 capability-dependent Windows cases skipped, and 14 subtests passed on the local Windows host
revalidation_evidence.affected_release_shard: 60 passed, 1 capability-dependent case skipped, and 30 subtests passed across installation, portable-content, portfolio, report-output, TestMesh installation-binding, and provenance tests
revalidation_evidence.full_local_snapshot: The complete local suite reached 469 passed, 2 skipped, and 146 subtests; its only two failures were v0.3.1 version-sync residues, and both affected version checks passed after correction
revalidation_evidence.validation_composition_model: 19/19 positive gates and 7/7 known-bad gates passed
revalidation_evidence.development_process_models: current runtime authority, portfolio scope lifecycle, global router, and runtime contract reviews passed with expected violations only
revalidation_evidence.contract_compilation: contract 325507B1F9D1D26EFB484AB950879EA828F1C239BABEB9D685E8470E54E990CC and manifest 0F140F0571CA948B3137CED347D174730E59A126FC1A43354464D7D5BD20D1FC compiled current with zero findings
revalidation_evidence.installed_parity: transaction install-e20fbe62e4994e42a2c081a22e33f154 committed as generation 36; installation receipt installation-05f5d47eb06c449e10782f97 replayed current canonical-to-active parity

claim_boundary: The repair has passed the named local regressions, executable model checks, deterministic compilation, and transactional installed parity. Pushed Windows/Ubuntu CI, the corrective tag, GitHub Release, future platform behavior, and unrelated target-domain correctness remain separate pending claims.
